q: Common forms of energy in daily life include heat, light, and electricity.
A non-renewable resource is a natural substance that is replenished faster than it is consumed.
Non-renewable energy resources include coal, natural gas, crude oil, and nuclear energy.
Non-renewable resources occur in limited and exhaustible quantities.
Renewable energy sources are also called non-conventional energy sources.
Renewable energy sources are continuously replenished by natural processes.
Renewable energy can be produced at a rate greater than or equal to the rate of consumption.
Renewable resources take millions of years to be replenished.
Wind, solar, and hydro are examples of renewable energy sources.
Renewable energy sources have a virtual unlimited supply.
